title: FIA Rule-State Stop-Debt Automaton
summary: The April 30, 2025 FIA Sporting Regulations encode hard strategic constraints that the current features only approximate: normal dry races require two dry specs, wet/intermediate usage changes that requirement, and Monaco 2025 uniquely requires three tyre sets.
rationale: The April 30, 2025 FIA Sporting Regulations encode hard strategic constraints that the current features only approximate: normal dry races require two dry specs, wet/intermediate usage changes that requirement, and Monaco 2025 uniquely requires three tyre sets. A deterministic "minimum stops still owed" state should help on late-race rows where pace says stay out but rules still force a stop.
implementation_hint: Build prior-only features per Driver x Race: slick compounds already used, pit-set count so far, current compound family, dry-vs-wet proxy, `must_use_new_compound`, `min_future_stops_owed`, `can_finish_but_owes_stop`, and a Monaco-2025-only branch for the three-set rule.
expected_effect: Sharper ranking near race end and fewer false negatives on mandatory strategic stops.
risk: Easy to misapply if Monaco 2025 rows are absent or if wet-race exemptions are encoded with future information; gate only on current-row race metadata and past stops.
